mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales indicates strengthening household demand and expected revenue growth, so higher RSAFS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot available before decision date was 344688.0
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS 3-month growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over a rolling 3-year window or if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating income growth and recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 6-month average by at least 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot available before decision date was 10.2
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while CPI is rising, real short rates fall and liquidity conditions improve, raising equity valuation multiples and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL has positive 3-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot available before decision date was 0.12
failure_condition: Reject if this joint condition produces average 60-day S&P 500 returns less than or equal to unconditional average returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weaker output and operating leverage pressure, so negative INDPRO momentum predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot available before decision date was 98.4622
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if triggered returns are not lower than baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts signals improving construction activity, credit availability, and durable-goods demand, so upward HOUST momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month average after being below it for at least 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot available before decision date was 590.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside crossings do not produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months, or if the hit rate of positive returns after triggers is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so positive FEDFUNDS momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if the FEDFUNDS momentum coefficient is nonnegative in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate confirm broad labor-market improvement, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot available before decision date was 130848.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor improvement condition has a 5-day forward hit rate below 50% or if its average 5-day return is not higher than the average return from PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: CPI acceleration combined with slowing industrial production indicates margin pressure and stagflation risk, leading investors to reduce equity exposure and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth is positive and INDPRO 3-month growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-up and INDPRO-down interaction is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if its return mean is not below the unconditional mean.
